messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "framework-agreements-hollow-large-operator-cell",
 "title": "Global framework agreements and bundled fit-outs foreclose the 0.40 large-operator cell the whole GTM is aimed at",
 "narrative": "The venture's stated first buyer is large operators, and cust.operator-large carries the biggest single channel weight (0.40) — a needs-source internal estimate with two-decimal precision. The corpus shows what that cell actually looks like from inside procurement: PDU spend at large operators flows through multi-year global framework agreements and integrated rack/power/cooling bundles (Digital Realty–Schneider style frameworks, all-in integrator contracts), not discrete PDU line items a regional entrant can bid. If half the 0.40 is procured this way, the model's most reachable-looking cell is structurally closed in Year 1 — and the conservative 1% obtainableFactor is being applied to a SAM that contains spend no entrant can contest at any price.",
 "category": "competitive",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.operator-large"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Large operators consolidate power-equipment purchasing into global frameworks and integrator bundles; Vertiv's own partner material lists hyperscalers, telcos and colo REITs as house accounts under standing agreements",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c12",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The 0.40 share was derived from 'hyperscale+colo ≈ 62% buy direct' — but the only quantitative datapoint behind the channel axis (a supplier's 30%-of-orders figure) measures DC exposure, not buyer-type contestability, and no source quantifies what fraction of 'direct' spend is framework-locked",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c0",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The contestable portion of the large-operator cell is materially below 0.40; SAM built on the full cell overstates reachable spend for the profile's chosen first buyer, and Year-1 pipeline math against it fails silently",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "'Large operators buy direct' is the founder's own GTM answer, and 0.40 looks like the biggest, most accessible cell. The surface appearance is a channel decision already made; what it hides is that 'direct' at these accounts means procured inside integrated frameworks the entrant cannot bid into — the cell exists, but not for them.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Three or more CE large-operator PDU procurements in the next 12 months run as discrete, multi-vendor bake-offs and won by vendors outside the incumbent framework holders.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "Every procurement case study retrieved (Jülich, Schneider–Foxconn, Iren–Dell, Digital Realty–Schneider) shows bundled or framework purchasing; no counter-example of a contested standalone PDU line at a large operator surfaced in any search pass. Uncertainty is about the foreclosed fraction, not the mechanism.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.18,
   "note": "Framework- and bundle-foreclosed portion (~half) of the direct large-operator cell removed from contestable spend; siblings deliberately not re-normalized — this is absolute shrinkage of reachable spend, not a mix-shift"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Structure of large-operator PDU procurement: discrete lots vs bundled fit-out packages",
   "where": "TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) filtered for PDU/rack-power CPV codes in DE/NL/PL; Digital Realty and Equinix supplier-announcement pages",
   "threshold": "<2 standalone, multi-bidder PDU lots from CE large operators over two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "New or renewed multi-year power-infrastructure framework agreements between colo REITs/hyperscalers and Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton",
   "where": "Schneider and Vertiv quarterly earnings calls and press rooms; operator capex-call commentary",
   "threshold": "≥2 new CE-relevant frameworks announced in 12 months",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Approved-vendor-list (AVL) openings at CE colocation operators",
   "where": "Operator supplier-qualification portals (Equinix, NTT, Data4, Atman)",
   "threshold": "Any AVL round explicitly inviting new rack-PDU vendors",
   "updates": "decreases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Run 5 structured procurement interviews at CE large operators to split the 0.40 cell into framework-locked vs contestable spend",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.operator-large",
   "note": "Directly replaces the unsourced 0.40 with an evidence-based contestable share — the single highest-leverage number for the stated GTM"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-sequence Year-1 GTM toward mid-market colo and enterprise retrofit, where discrete PDU purchasing is documented, and treat large operators as a Year-2+ AVL-qualification play",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Aligns the ramp with the cells that are actually contestable pre-brand"
  },
  {
   "action": "Pursue sub-supplier status inside one integrator's bundle (rack integrator or fit-out contractor) rather than competing against the bundle",
   "type": "contractual",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Converts the foreclosure mechanism into a channel"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "ledger-self-audit",
 "riskId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.framework-agreements-hollow-large-operator-cell"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

operator capex-call commentary","threshold":"≥2 new CE-relevant frameworks announced in 12 months","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Approved-vendor-list (AVL) openings at CE colocation operators","where":"Operator supplier-qualification portals (Equinix, NTT, Data4, Atman)","threshold":"Any AVL round explicitly inviting new rack-PDU vendors","updates":"decreases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Run 5 structured procurement interviews at CE large operators to split the 0.40 cell into framework-locked vs contestable spend","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.operator-large","note":"Directly replaces the unsourced 0.40 with an evidence-based contestable share — the single highest-leverage number for the stated GTM"},{"action":"Re-sequence Year-1 GTM toward mid-market colo and enterprise retrofit, where discrete PDU purchasing is documented, and treat large operators as a Year-2+ AVL-qualification play","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Aligns the ramp with the cells that are actually contestable pre-brand"},{"action":"Pursue sub-supplier status inside one integrator's bundle (rack integrator or fit-out contractor) rather than competing against the bundle","type":"contractual","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Converts the foreclosure mechanism into a channel"}]}
